articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

These moments of alignment, known as eclipses, occultations, and transits, allow scientists to study distant objects in remarkable ways. By observing how light change...
articles.takeaway: Celestial alignment observation techniques used in astronomy inform precision navigation and timing systems critical for aviation operations.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Airlines can reduce environmental impact by 20-30% through operational optimization alone, without waiting for next-generation aircraft technology deployment.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster

articles.takeaway: The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers in a mid-air collision, becoming a watershed moment for military aviation safety protocols and airfield operations procedures.

articles.headline: See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami

articles.takeaway: GUARDIAN's real-time detection of ionospheric disturbances from satellites could provide critical minutes of advance warning before traditional seismic systems, fundamentally improving disaster response coordination for coastal aviation operations and airports.
